revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24699703697|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:波自加弥神社]] {{Expand Japanese|波自加弥神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hajikami Shrine | native_name = 波自加弥神社 | image = Hajikamijinja.JPG | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hajikami Shrine | coordinates = {{coord|36.634392|136.71499}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] }} {{nihongo|'''Hajikami Shrine'''|波自加弥神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kaga Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11399659|ltq=Q11399659|qid=Q11399659|1=List of Shikinaisha in Kaga Province}} ** Series ordinal: 39 ** P155: {{ill|Notsuchino Shrine|lt=Notsuchino Shrine|qid=Q135040297|1=Notsuchino Shrine}} ** P156: {{ill|Q11439679|ltq=Q11439679|qid=Q11439679|1=Onominato Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * کاگا|fi|Kagan provinssi|fr|Province de Kaga|gl|Provincia de Kaga|id|Provinsi Kaga|it|Kaga (provincia)|ja|加賀国|ko|가가국|lt|Kagos provincija|nl|Kaga (provincie)|pt|Província de Kaga|ru|Кага (провинция)|simple|Kaga Province|sk|Kaga (provincia)|sr|Провинција Кага|tr|Kaga (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Каґа|zh_min_nan|Kaga-kok|zh_yue|加賀國|zh|加賀國|qid=Q907469|1=Kaga Province}} * {{ill|Q1130942|ltq=Q1130942|qid=Q1130942|1=Kahoku District, Ishikawa}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|36.634392|136.71499}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == image (P18) == * Hajikamijinja.JPG == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 182340 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Q134917316|ltq=Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * ᥓᥣᥱ ᥙᥣᥢᥱ|tet|Japaun|te|జపాన్|tg|Ҷопон|th|ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|thnews|หมวดหมู่:ประเทศญี่ปุ่น|tig|ጃፓን|ti|ጃፓን|tk|Ýaponiýa|tl|Hapon|tly|Japonijə|tn|Japan|tok|ma Nijon|to|Siapani|tpi|Siapan|trv|Tanah Tunux|tr|Japonya|trnews|Kategori:Japonya|trquote|Japonya|trvoyage|Japonya|ts|Japani|tt|Япония|tum|Japan|tw|Japan|tyv|Япония|ty|Tāpōnē|udm|Япония|ug|ياپونىيە|uk|Японія|uknews|Категорія:Японія|ukquote|Японія|ukvoyage|Японія|ur|جاپان|uz|Yaponiya|vec|Japon|vep|Japonii|ve|Japan|vi|Nhật Bản|vibooks|Các quốc gia/Nhật Bản|vivoyage|Nhật Bản|vls|Japan|vo|Yapän|war|Hapon|wa|Djapon|wo|Sapoŋ|wuu|日本|xal|Ниxуудин Нутг|xh|IJapani|xmf|იაპონია|yi|יאפאן|yo|Japan|za|Nyiedbonj|zea|Japan|zgh|ⵏⵉⴱⵓⵏ|zh_classical|日本|zh_min_nan|Ji̍t-pún|zh_yue|日本|zh|日本|zhnews|Category:日本|zhquote|日本|zhvoyage|日本|zu|IJaphani|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == name in kana (P1814) == * はじかみじんじゃ == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5220005001099 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '石川県金沢市二日市町カ186番地', 'language': 'ja'} * {'text': '石川県金沢市花園八幡町', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:波自加弥神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11555598}} == Japanese content == {{?? |?? = Hajikami Shrine |?? = [[??:Hajikamijinja.JPG|250px]]
Torii |??? = {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|qid=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}} Futsukaichi-machi Ka 186 |?? = {{?????2????|36|38|3.35|N|136|42|53.67|E|region:JP-17_type:landmark_scale:25000|display=inline,title}} |?? = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hajikami-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}?{{ill|lt=Hachiman|Q261637|qid=Q261637|lt=Sei-Hachiman-no-Kami|1=Hachiman}} |?? = former {{ill|lt=G?-sha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}?{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (small) |?? = {{ill|lt=Y?r?|Q1373061|qid=Q1373061|1=Yōrō}} 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|qid=Q23534|1=718}}) |?? = |?? = |?? = {{ill|lt=April 11|Q2501|qid=Q2501|1=April 11}} |?? = |?? = {{Location map|Japan Ishikawa|label=????
??|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=10|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hajikami Shrine''' (????????; formally '''??????''') is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} at Futsukaichi-machi Ka 186, {{ill|lt=Kanazawa|Q191130|qid=Q191130|1=Kanazawa}}, {{ill|lt=Ishikawa Prefecture|Q131281|qid=Q131281|1=Ishikawa Prefecture}}. === Overview === It is located in the northern part of Kanazawa. Irrigation water runs near the torii at the front approach, where a monument honors the builder of the canal. The precinct is covered by dense woodland, with an uphill approach leading to the shrine buildings. According to ''Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Records,'' the enshrined deities are Hajikami-no-Kami and Sei-Hachiman-no-Kami. However, a local monument lists many deities: Hajikami-no-Kami, {{ill|lt=Amaterasu|Q455602|qid=Q455602|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor ?jin|Q317997|qid=Q317997|1=Emperor Ōjin}}, {{ill|lt=Empress Jing?|Q232803|qid=Q232803|1=Empress Jingū}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Ch?ai|Q179971|qid=Q179971|1=Emperor Chūai}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hime-no-?kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Sukunabikona-no-Kami|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q1502690|lt=Takemikazuchi-no-Kami|1=Takemikazuchi}}, {{ill|qid=Q1107286|lt=Futsunushi-no-Kami|Q1107286|qid=Q1107286|1=Futsunushi}}, {{ill|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Koyane-no-Kami}}, {{ill|lt=Yasakatome-no-Kami|Q9150392|qid=Q9150392|1=Yasakatome}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q9150391|lt=Takeminakata-no-Kami|1=Takeminakata}}. There is also the Shoichii Hachimanyama Inari-sha within the precinct, and a sessha called Jinbeid? enshrining {{ill|lt=Takenouchi no Sukune|Q1073668|qid=Q1073668|1=Takenouchi no Sukune}}. According to chief priest Tajika Akitsugu, the shrine name comes from the old word "hajikami" for pungent things chewed with the teeth (ginger, wasabi, sansh?, etc.){{Cite web|??|title='Nice to meet you! I am the chief priest of Hajikami Shrine'|url=https://ameblo.jp/hajikamijinja/entry-10288587952.html|website=Chief priest's blog|accessdate=2020-11-25|language=ja|date=2009-06-27}}. === History === Founded in Y?r? 2 ({{ill|lt=718|Q23534|qid=Q23534|1=718}}). In the ''{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|qid=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}'', it was listed as a national small shrine. During the Genpei Hokuriku campaign in Juei 2 ({{ill|lt=1183|Q19723|qid=Q19723|1=1183}}), all buildings burned in warfare. It was moved to Tajika Hachiman-g? at the current site and became a combined shrine, then later renamed to its current name because Hajikami-no-Kami, with Senior Fourth Rank, was considered above Hachiman. The original site was at Kogane Sh?zu in Shib? Takasaka-ch?, about 2 km further inland. In Taish? 14 ({{ill|lt=1925|Q18107|qid=Q18107|1=1925}}), it became a G?-sha. === Kogane Sh?zu spring === At the old shrine site in Shib? Takasaka-ch?, the spring called Kogane Sh?zu is tied to a legend: during severe drought in the Nara period, the kuni no miyatsuko prayed at this shrine for rain. On the 37th day, sacred water sprang forth and saved the people. With little left to offer, they dedicated ginger, beginning the unique "ginger festival" still held each year on {{ill|lt=June 15|Q2613|qid=Q2613|1=June 15}}. In the Edo period, restaurant owners from Kaga, Etch?, and Noto reportedly visited. Because of ginger's medicinal properties, it also became a place of healing worship. This continues todayNagatanien, active in ginger research/product development, registered June 15 as "Ginger Day" with the Japan Anniversary Association in connection with this grand festival.. === Access === * Hokutetsu Kanazawa Bus: from {{ill|lt=Morimoto Station|Q5359635|qid=Q5359635|lt=Morimoto Station|1=Morimoto Station}}, route 87 via Hachiman to Moto-Tsubata; get off at Hachiman stop, 3 min walk ** For the distant worship hall: route 87 via Futsukaichi to Moto-Tsubata; get off at Futsukaichi (free boarding section; can board/alight near main gate, but service is limited) === Notes === === References === * ''Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Records,'' edited by Ishikawa Prefecture Shrine Office (1976) * Based on one stone monument and one standing notice in the shrine precinct (authors: Akitsugu Tajika / unknown; dated Jan 1985 / undated) {{?? ?}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Hajikami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Hajikami Shrine]][[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanazawa, Ishikawa]] [[Category:金沢市の歴史]] [[Category:石川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kaga Province]][[Category:Shinto shrines in Ishikawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Kanazawa, Ishikawa]]
